lede: A look at March 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Steve Mandanda and Fra Bartolomeo, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2025 Myanmar earthquake
events.desc: An earthquake strikes close to Mandalay, Myanmar with a magnitude of 7.7, killing over 5400 people.

events.title: Uusimaa
events.desc: The region of Uusimaa (with the capital city Helsinki) is temporarily isolated from the rest of Finland due to increased COVID-19 infections.

events.title: Kosovo War
events.desc: Kosovo War: Serb paramilitary and military forces kill at least 130 Kosovo Albanians in Izbica.

events.title: Civil rights movement
events.desc: Civil rights movement: Over one hundred high school students conduct a sit-in protest in Rome, Georgia.

events.title: Cold War
events.desc: Cold War: The United States Department of State releases the Acheson–Lilienthal Report, outlining a plan for the international control of nuclear power.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: First day of the Battle of Cape Matapan in Greece between the navies of the United Kingdom and Australia, and the Royal Italian navy.

events.title: Spanish Civil War
events.desc: Spanish Civil War: Generalissimo Francisco Franco conquers Madrid after a three-year siege.

events.title: Imperial Airways
events.desc: The Imperial Airways biplane City of Liverpool is believed to be the first airliner lost to sabotage when a passenger sets a fire on board.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: In the Battle of Glorieta Pass, Union forces stop the Confederate invasion of the New Mexico Territory. The battle began on March 26.

events.title: Heinrich Wilhelm Matthias Olbers
events.desc: Heinrich Wilhelm Matthäus Olbers discovers 2 Pallas, the second asteroid ever to be discovered.

births.name: Maxim Gorky
births.line: Russian novelist, short story writer, and playwright

births.name: Mario Vargas Llosa
births.line: Peruvian writer and politician, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Laura Chinchilla
births.line: Costa Rican politician, 46th President of Costa Rica

deaths.text: Peter Ustinov, English-Swiss actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Dwight D. Eisenhower, American general and politician, 34th President of the United States
